file.content:
---
description: "Dispatches a single roadmap step to a specialized agent for TDD execution. Use when implementing a specific step from a roadmap.yaml plan."
argument-hint: '[agent] [project-id] [step-id] - Example: @nw-software-crafter "auth-upgrade" "01-01"'
---

# NW-EXECUTE: Atomic Task Execution

**Wave**: EXECUTION_WAVE | **Agent**: Dispatched agent (specified by caller)

## Overview

Dispatch a single roadmap step to an agent. Orchestrator extracts step context from roadmap so agent never loads the full roadmap.

## Syntax

```
/nw:execute @{agent} "{project-id}" "{step-id}"
```

## Context Files Required

- `docs/feature/{project-id}/roadmap.yaml` — Orchestrator reads once, extracts step context
- `docs/feature/{project-id}/execution-log.yaml` — Agent appends only (never reads)

## Rigor Profile Integration

Before dispatching the agent, read rigor config from `.nwave/des-config.json` (key: `rigor`). If absent, use standard defaults.

- **`agent_model`**: Pass as `model` parameter to Task tool. If `"inherit"`, omit `model` (inherits from session).
- **`tdd_phases`**: If `["RED_UNIT", "GREEN"]` (lean), modify the TDD_PHASES section in the DES template to only include those 2 phases. Remove PREPARE/RED_ACCEPTANCE/COMMIT instructions.
- **`refactor_pass`**: If `false`, skip COMMIT phase refactoring instructions.

## Dispatcher Workflow

1. Parse parameters: agent name|project ID|step ID
2. Read rigor profile from `.nwave/des-config.json` (default: standard)
3. Validate roadmap and execution-log exist
4. Grep roadmap for `step_id: "{step-id}"` with ~50 lines context
5. Extract step fields and invoke Task tool with DES template below, applying rigor model and phases

## Agent Invocation

@{agent}

Use this DES template verbatim. Fill `{placeholders}` from roadmap. Without DES markers, hooks cannot validate.

```
<!-- DES-VALIDATION : required -->
<!-- DES-PROJECT-ID : {project-id} -->
<!-- DES-STEP-ID : {step-id} -->

# DES_METADATA
Step: {step-id}
Project: {project-id}
Command: /nw:execute

# AGENT_IDENTITY
Agent: {agent-name}

# SKILL_LOADING
Before starting TDD phases, read your skill files for methodology guidance.
Skills path: ~/.claude/skills/nw/{agent-name}/
Always load at PREPARE: tdd-methodology.md, quality-framework.md
Load on-demand per phase as specified in your Skill Loading Strategy table.

# TASK_CONTEXT
{step context from roadmap - name|description|acceptance_criteria|test_file|scenario_line|acceptance_test_scenario|quality_gates|implementation_notes|dependencies|estimated_hours|deliverables}

# TDD_PHASES
Execute in order:
0. PREPARE - Load context, verify prerequisites
1. RED_ACCEPTANCE - Write failing acceptance test
2. RED_UNIT - Write failing unit test
3. GREEN - Minimal code to pass tests
   After GREEN: run FULL test suite. If all pass, proceed to COMMIT immediately.
   Never move to new task or stop without committing green code.
4. COMMIT - Stage and commit with conventional message
   Include git trailer: `Step-ID: {step-id}` (required for DES verification)
   Example:
   ```
   feat(project-id): implement feature X

   Step-ID: 02-01
   ```

# QUALITY_GATES
- All tests pass before COMMIT
- No skipped phases without blocked_by reason
- Coverage maintained or improved

# OUTCOME_RECORDING
After ACTUALLY EXECUTING each phase, record via DES CLI:

    PYTHONPATH=$HOME/.claude/lib/python python -m des.cli.log_phase \
      --project-dir docs/feature/{project-id} \
      --step-id {step-id} \
      --phase {PHASE_NAME} \
      --status EXECUTED \
      --data PASS

For SKIPPED phases (genuinely not applicable):

    PYTHONPATH=$HOME/.claude/lib/python python -m des.cli.log_phase \
      --project-dir docs/feature/{project-id} \
      --step-id {step-id} \
      --phase {PHASE_NAME} \
      --status SKIPPED \
      --data "NOT_APPLICABLE: reason"

CLI enforces real UTC timestamps and validates phase names.
Do NOT manually edit execution-log.yaml.

CRITICAL: Only the executing agent calls the CLI.
Orchestrator MUST NEVER write phase entries — only the agent that performed the work. A log entry without actual execution is fraud.

# BOUNDARY_RULES
- Only modify files listed in step's files_to_modify
- Do not load roadmap.yaml
- Do not modify execution-log.yaml structure (append only)
- NEVER write execution-log entries for phases you did not execute

# TIMEOUT_INSTRUCTION
Target: 30 turns max. If approaching limit, COMMIT current progress.
If GREEN complete (all tests pass), MUST commit before returning — even at turn limit.
```

**Configuration:**
- subagent_type: extracted agent name
- max_turns by step complexity:

| Step Type | Typical Tool Calls | Recommended max_turns |
|-----------|-------------------|----------------------|
| Hotfix (1 file) | 10-12 | 25 |
| Standard TDD (2-3 files) | 25-30 | 45 |
| Complex (4+ files, new module) | 35-45 | 65 |

Default: 45. Heuristic: `20 + (files_to_modify count * 8)`, capped at 65.

## Error Handling

- Invalid agent: report available agents
- Missing roadmap/execution-log: report path not found
- Step not in roadmap: report available step IDs
- Dependency failure: explain blocking tasks

## Resume vs Restart

When subagent times out:

| Last Completed Phase | Action | Rationale |
|---------------------|--------|-----------|
| GREEN (or later) | Resume | Only COMMIT remains (~5 turns) |
| RED_UNIT with partial GREEN | Resume | Preserves implementation progress |
| PREPARE or RED_ACCEPTANCE | Restart | Little context worth replaying |

Resume costs ~50% more tokens/call due to context replay (measured: 3.7K vs 2.5K tokens/call). For <5 remaining turns, resume is efficient. For 15+ turns, restart with higher max_turns is cheaper.

## Examples

```bash
/nw:execute @nw-software-crafter "des-us007-boundary-rules" "02-01"
/nw:execute @nw-researcher "auth-upgrade" "01-01"
/nw:execute @nw-software-crafter "des-us007" "03-01"  # retry after failure
```

## TDD_PHASES
<!-- Schema v4.0 — canonical source: TDDPhaseValidator.MANDATORY_PHASES -->
<!-- Build system injects mandatory phases from step-tdd-cycle-schema.json -->
{{MANDATORY_PHASES}}

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Agent invoked via Task tool (dispatcher does not execute the work)
- [ ] Step context extracted from roadmap and passed in prompt
- [ ] Agent appended phase events to execution-log.yaml
- [ ] Agent did not load roadmap.yaml

## Next Wave

**Handoff To**: /nw:review for post-execution review
**Deliverables**: Updated execution-log.yaml|implementation artifacts|git commits

content.text: Roadmap approved. Now executing Phase 2 — all steps in dependency order. Starting with step 01-01:

input.prompt:
<!-- DES-VALIDATION : required -->
<!-- DES-PROJECT-ID : learning-library -->
<!-- DES-STEP-ID : 01-01 -->

# DES_METADATA
Step: 01-01
Project: learning-library
Command: /nw:execute

# AGENT_IDENTITY
Agent: nw-functional-software-crafter

# SKILL_LOADING
Before starting TDD phases, read your skill files for methodology guidance.
Skills path: ~/.claude/skills/nw/nw-functional-software-crafter/
Always load at PREPARE: tdd-methodology.md, quality-framework.md
Load on-demand per phase as specified in your Skill Loading Strategy table.

# TASK_CONTEXT
Name: Shared contracts, learnings route, and data hooks
Description: Add KNOWN_LEARNING_TARGET_AGENTS constant to shared contracts. Register /learnings route in TanStack Router under authLayout. Create use-learnings hook (fetch + filter state) and use-learning-actions hook (mutations).

Files to modify:
- app/src/shared/contracts.ts
- app/src/client/router.tsx
- app/src/client/hooks/use-learnings.ts
- app/src/client/hooks/use-learning-actions.ts

Acceptance Criteria:
- KNOWN_LEARNING_TARGET_AGENTS exported from contracts with all agent types
- Navigating to /learnings renders a page component without errors
- use-learnings hook fetches learnings from GET endpoint with status/type/agent params
- use-learning-actions hook exposes approve, dismiss, deactivate, create, and edit mutations
- Edit mutation calls PUT endpoint; other mutations call existing POST endpoints

Dependencies: none

Existing acceptance tests: tests/acceptance/learning-library/walking-skeleton.test.ts, tests/acceptance/learning-library/milestone-1-browse-filter.test.ts

## Key Patterns to Follow

**Router pattern** (from existing router.tsx):
- Use `createRoute({ getParentRoute: () => authLayout, path: '/learnings', component: LearningsPage })`
- Add to routeTree in router

**Hook pattern** (from existing useGovernanceFeed):
- useState + useEffect + fetch (NO React Query)
- Return `{ data, isLoading, error, refresh }`
- Use `useWorkspaceState(s => s.workspaceId)` for workspace context

**Contracts** (from existing shared/contracts.ts):
- Export typed constant array: `KNOWN_LEARNING_TARGET_AGENTS`
- Each entry: `{ value: string, label: string }`
- Values: "chat_agent", "pm_agent", "observer_agent", "mcp"

**API endpoints:**
- GET /api/workspaces/:wsId/learnings?status=X&type=Y&agent=Z (list)
- POST /api/workspaces/:wsId/learnings (create with collision detection)
- POST /api/workspaces/:wsId/learnings/:id/actions (approve/dismiss/deactivate)
- PUT /api/workspaces/:wsId/learnings/:id (edit — to be built in step 04-01)

**Existing LearningSummary type** (in contracts.ts):
```typescript
type LearningSummary = {
  id: string; text: string;
  learningType: "constraint" | "instruction" | "precedent";
  status: "active" | "pending_approval" | "dismissed" | "superseded" | "deactivated";
  source: "human" | "agent";
  priority: EntityPriority;
  targetAgents: string[];
  suggestedBy?: string; patternConfidence?: number;
  createdAt: string; approvedAt?: string;
  dismissedAt?: string; dismissedReason?: string; deactivatedAt?: string;
};
```

**Data value contract**: Never use `null`. Use `undefined` via optional properties.
**Paradigm**: Functional — pure functions, composition, types as documentation.

# TDD_PHASES
Execute in order:
0. PREPARE - Load context, verify prerequisites, read existing files
1. RED_ACCEPTANCE - Write failing acceptance test (or verify existing tests fail as expected)
2. RED_UNIT - Write failing unit test for the hooks/contracts
3. GREEN - Minimal code to pass tests
   After GREEN: run FULL test suite. If all pass, proceed to COMMIT immediately.
   Never move to new task or stop without committing green code.
4. COMMIT - Stage and commit with conventional message
   Include git trailer: `Step-ID: 01-01` (required for DES verification)

# QUALITY_GATES
- All tests pass before COMMIT
- No skipped phases without blocked_by reason
- Coverage maintained or improved

# OUTCOME_RECORDING
After ACTUALLY EXECUTING each phase, record via DES CLI:

    PYTHONPATH=$HOME/.claude/lib/python python -m des.cli.log_phase \
      --project-dir docs/feature/learning-library \
      --step-id 01-01 \
      --phase {PHASE_NAME} \
      --status EXECUTED \
      --data PASS

For SKIPPED phases:

    PYTHONPATH=$HOME/.claude/lib/python python -m des.cli.log_phase \
      --project-dir docs/feature/learning-library \
      --step-id 01-01 \
      --phase {PHASE_NAME} \
      --status SKIPPED \
      --data "NOT_APPLICABLE: reason"

# BOUNDARY_RULES
- Only modify files listed in files_to_modify (plus test files)
- Do not load roadmap.yaml
- Do not modify execution-log.yaml structure (append only via CLI)
- NEVER write execution-log entries for phases you did not execute
- Use `--no-verify` and `-s` flags when committing (per AGENTS.md)

# TIMEOUT_INSTRUCTION
Target: 30 turns max. If approaching limit, COMMIT current progress.
If GREEN complete (all tests pass), MUST commit before returning.

- app/src/server/learning/learning-route.ts
- app/src/server/learning/queries.ts
- app/src/server/runtime/start-server.ts

Acceptance Criteria:
- PUT returns 200 with updated fields on valid edit of active learning
- PUT returns 409 when learning is not in active status
- PUT returns 404 for non-existent or wrong-workspace learning
- PUT returns 400 for empty or whitespace-only text
- Text edit triggers embedding regeneration

Dependencies: 01-01 (completed — shared contracts exist)

## Key Patterns
- Read existing learning-route.ts to understand the handler patterns (handleCreate, handleList, handleAction)
- Read existing queries.ts to understand query patterns (updateLearningText, updateLearningStatus)
- Read start-server.ts to understand route registration pattern
- The PUT handler should follow the same pattern as handleCreate/handleAction
- Use workspace scope validation (same as other learning endpoints)
- Re-embed text via the same embedding function used in handleCreate
- Data value contract: Never use `null`, use `undefined` via optional properties
- Paradigm: functional
- SurrealDB: use RecordId objects, not string IDs

## Existing Tests to Unskip
- tests/acceptance/learning-library/milestone-2-edit-learning.test.ts has 12 scenarios marked `it.skip` — these test the PUT endpoint. After implementing, unskip them and verify they pass.

Goal: Learning Library UI — browse/filter learnings, inline pending actions, edit/deactivate active learnings, create new learnings with agent targeting.

## Architecture Context

### Component Boundaries (13 new files, 5 modified)

**New files:**
- `app/src/client/routes/learnings-page.tsx` — page component
- `app/src/client/hooks/use-learnings.ts` — data fetching + filter state
- `app/src/client/hooks/use-learning-actions.ts` — mutations
- `app/src/client/hooks/use-pending-learning-count.ts` — sidebar badge
- `app/src/client/components/learning/LearningList.tsx` — card list
- `app/src/client/components/learning/LearningCard.tsx` — expandable card
- `app/src/client/components/learning/LearningFilters.tsx` — dropdowns
- `app/src/client/components/learning/StatusTabs.tsx` — tab bar
- `app/src/client/components/learning/ApproveDialog.tsx` — approve + edit + collision
- `app/src/client/components/learning/DismissDialog.tsx` — dismiss + reason
- `app/src/client/components/learning/EditDialog.tsx` — edit fields
- `app/src/client/components/learning/DeactivateDialog.tsx` — confirmation
- `app/src/client/components/learning/CreateDialog.tsx` — create form + collision
- `app/src/client/components/learning/AgentChips.tsx` — agent badges

**Modified files:**
- `app/src/client/router.tsx` — add /learnings route
- `app/src/client/components/layout/WorkspaceSidebar.tsx` — add nav link + badge
- `app/src/shared/contracts.ts` — add KNOWN_LEARNING_TARGET_AGENTS constant
- `app/src/server/learning/learning-route.ts` — add PUT handler
- `app/src/server/learning/queries.ts` — add updateLearningFields
- `app/src/server/runtime/start-server.ts` — register PUT route

### User Stories (implementation order)
1. US-LL-01: Browse & Filter — list all learnings, filter by status/type/agent (foundation)
2. US-LL-04: Create Learning — dialog with type, priority, agent targeting, collision feedback
3. US-LL-02: Inline Pending Actions — approve/dismiss buttons on pending cards
4. US-LL-03: Edit/Deactivate — edit text/priority/agents, deactivate with confirmation

### Backend API (all existing except PUT)
- GET /api/workspaces/:wsId/learnings?status=X&type=Y&agent=Z
- POST /api/workspaces/:wsId/learnings (create with collision detection)
- POST /api/workspaces/:wsId/learnings/:id/actions (approve/dismiss/deactivate)
- PUT /api/workspaces/:wsId/learnings/:id (NEW — edit active learnings)

### Existing Test Suites
- `tests/acceptance/learning-library/walking-skeleton.test.ts`
- `tests/acceptance/learning-library/milestone-1-browse-filter.test.ts`
- `tests/acceptance/learning-library/milestone-2-edit-learning.test.ts` (it.skip — blocked on PUT)
- `tests/acceptance/learning-library/milestone-3-full-lifecycle.test.ts`

### Key Patterns
- React + TanStack Router + Zustand + vanilla CSS (BEM)
- Data hooks: useState + useEffect + fetch (no React Query)
- No optimistic updates — refetch-on-success
- No new Zustand store — hook-local state
- Paradigm: functional

## Phase Mapping

Phase 01: Foundation (shared contracts, route, hooks, sidebar)
Phase 02: Browse & Filter UI (tabs, filters, list, cards)
Phase 03: Actions (approve/dismiss/create dialogs)
Phase 04: Edit/Deactivate (PUT endpoint, edit/deactivate dialogs)

## Rules for Filling

- Each step should modify 1-4 production files (decomposition ratio ≤ 2.5)
- Acceptance criteria must be behavioral and testable
- Step IDs are already set — do NOT change them
- Include `files_to_modify` for each step
- Include `depends_on` for dependencies between steps
- Update `implementation_scope` with actual directories
- Set `validation.status` to "pending"

1→---
     2→name: roadmap-design
     3→description: Roadmap concision rules, step decomposition efficiency, AC abstraction guidelines, and step-to-scenario mapping. Load when creating implementation roadmaps.
     4→---
     5→
     6→# Roadmap Design
     7→
     8→## Canonical Format
     9→
    10→Use ONLY compact nested format. See `nWave/templates/roadmap-schema.yaml` for fields/validation. Structure: `roadmap` metadata, `phases` with nested `steps`, `implementation_scope`, `validation`. Phase IDs: two digits (`"01"`). Step IDs: `"NN-MM"`.
    11→
    12→## Concision Requirements
    13→
    14→Token efficiency: crafter reads roadmap ~35 times (7 phases x 5 steps). 5000-token x 35 = 175k tokens. 1000-token x 35 = 35k (80% savings).
    15→
    16→### Quantitative Limits
    17→- Step description: max 50 words | AC per step: max 5 | Each AC: max 30 words
    18→- Step notes: max 100 words
    19→- Target: small (1-3 steps) = 500, medium (4-8) = 1500, large (9-15) = 3000 tokens
    20→
    21→### Include
    22→WHAT to build (one sentence) | WHY (brief business value) | Observable outcomes (testable AC) | Architectural constraints | Integration points
    23→
    24→### Exclude
    25→HOW to implement | code snippets/class names | algorithm descriptions | step-by-step instructions | tech tutorials | testing strategy (TDD standard) | motivational language | redundancy | examples when description clear
    26→
    27→### Compression Techniques
    28→Bullets over prose | eliminate qualifiers | assume expertise | active voice | omit obvious
    29→
    30→## Acceptance Criteria Abstraction
    31→
    32→AC describe observable outcomes, never internal implementation.
    33→
    34→### Rules
    35→Describe WHAT (behavior) not HOW (implementation) | Never reference private methods (underscore prefix) | Never reference internal class decomposition | Never prescribe signatures/params/return types | Crafter decides structure during GREEN + REFACTOR
    36→
    37→### Good AC Examples
    38→- "DES module importable from installation target after install"
    39→- "DES utility scripts present and executable in scripts directory"
    40→- "Installation uses backup before overwriting existing components"
    41→
    42→### Bad AC Examples (implementation-coupled)
    43→- "_install_des_module() copies src/des/ to ~/.claude/lib/python/des/"
    44→- "verify() checks DES module importable via subprocess test"
    45→- "context.installation_verifier._check_agents() called for verification"
    46→
    47→## Step Decomposition Efficiency
    48→
    49→### Step Ratio Check
    50→Rule: `steps_count / estimated_production_files <= 2.5`
    51→Violation = over-decomposition. Action: merge steps targeting same file.
    52→
    53→### Identical Pattern Detection
    54→Rule: if N steps differ only by substitution variable, batch into 1 step. Threshold: 3+ identical-pattern steps must batch.
    55→Bad: 4 separate steps for AgentsPlugin, CommandsPlugin, TemplatesPlugin, UtilitiesPlugin
    56→Good: 1 step creating all 4
    57→
    58→### No-Op Prevention
    59→Each step must add production code. Validation-only steps are not steps.
    60→Bad: "Step 02-05: Validate extraction with integration tests"
    61→Good: validation is part of preceding step's REVIEW phase
    62→
    63→## Step-to-Scenario Mapping
    64→
    65→For roadmaps feeding DELIVER wave:
    66→1. Read acceptance tests (tests/acceptance/test_*.py) before creating roadmap
    67→2. Count scenarios (def test_*) | 3. ~1 step per scenario (flexibility for infra steps)
    68→4. Mark infra steps: "type: infrastructure" | 5. Principle: 1 Scenario = 1 Step = 1 TDD Cycle
    69→
    70→## Measure Before Plan Gate
    71→
    72→Before any roadmap, verify: 1. Timing data shows WHERE time spent | 2. Impact ranking shows MOST contributing component | 3. Target validation provides evidence
    73→
    74→Missing data: halt, request measurement, offer to help. Gate is blocking.
    75→
    76→## Simplest Solution Gate
    77→
    78→Before multi-phase (>3 steps), document rejected alternatives:
    79→
    80→### Alternatives to Consider
    81→1. Configuration-only (no code) | 2. Single-file change | 3. Existing tool/library | 4. Partial (solve 80% simply)
    82→
    83→### Documentation Format
    84→```markdown
    85→## Rejected Simple Alternatives
    86→
    87→### Alternative 1: {simplest approach}
    88→- What: {description}
    89→- Expected Impact: {% of problem solved}
    90→- Why Insufficient: {evidence-based reason}
    91→
    92→### Why Complex Solution Necessary
    93→1. Simple alternatives fail due to: {specific reason with evidence}
    94→2. Complexity justified by: {benefit simple solutions cannot achieve}
    95→```
    96→
    97→Min 2 alternatives. Each: specific description, expected impact, evidence-based rejection.
    98→

input.content:
roadmap:
  project_id: learning-library
  created_at: '2026-03-13T10:46:06Z'
  total_steps: 10
  phases: 4
phases:
- id: '01'
  name: 'Foundation: contracts, routing, data hooks, sidebar'
  steps:
  - id: 01-01
    name: 'Shared contracts, learnings route, and data hooks'
    description: >
      Add KNOWN_LEARNING_TARGET_AGENTS constant to shared contracts.
      Register /learnings route in TanStack Router under authLayout.
      Create use-learnings hook (fetch + filter state) and use-learning-actions hook (mutations).
    files_to_modify:
      - app/src/shared/contracts.ts
      - app/src/client/router.tsx
      - app/src/client/hooks/use-learnings.ts
      - app/src/client/hooks/use-learning-actions.ts
    depends_on: []
    criteria:
      - 'KNOWN_LEARNING_TARGET_AGENTS exported from contracts with all agent types'
      - 'Navigating to /learnings renders a page component without errors'
      - 'use-learnings hook fetches learnings from GET endpoint with status/type/agent params'
      - 'use-learning-actions hook exposes approve, dismiss, deactivate, and create mutations'
      - 'Mutations call correct endpoints and trigger refetch on success'
  - id: 01-02
    name: 'Sidebar navigation link with pending count badge'
    description: >
      Add Learnings nav link to WorkspaceSidebar between Graph and Projects.
      Create use-pending-learning-count hook that polls pending_approval count.
      Display count badge on sidebar link when pending > 0.
    files_to_modify:
      - app/src/client/components/layout/WorkspaceSidebar.tsx
      - app/src/client/hooks/use-pending-learning-count.ts
    depends_on:
      - 01-01
    criteria:
      - 'Sidebar shows Learnings link that navigates to /learnings'
      - 'Active route highlights the Learnings link'
      - 'Badge displays pending count when workspace has pending learnings'
      - 'Badge hidden when pending count is zero'
- id: '02'
  name: 'Browse and Filter UI'
  steps:
  - id: 02-01
    name: 'Learnings page shell with StatusTabs'
    description: >
      Create learnings-page route component with page layout.
      Create StatusTabs component showing Active, Pending, Dismissed, Deactivated tabs.
      Active tab selected by default; switching tabs updates filter state in use-learnings.
    files_to_modify:
      - app/src/client/routes/learnings-page.tsx
      - app/src/client/components/learning/StatusTabs.tsx
    depends_on:
      - 01-01
    criteria:
      - 'Page renders four status tabs: Active, Pending, Dismissed, Deactivated'
      - 'Active tab selected by default on page load'
      - 'Switching tabs triggers refetch with corresponding status filter'
      - 'Each tab displays its learning count'
  - id: 02-02
    name: 'LearningFilters: type and agent dropdowns'
    description: >
      Create LearningFilters component with type dropdown (constraint/instruction/precedent)
      and agent dropdown (populated from KNOWN_LEARNING_TARGET_AGENTS).
      Filters compose with status tab selection.
    files_to_modify:
      - app/src/client/components/learning/LearningFilters.tsx
    depends_on:
      - 02-01
    criteria:
      - 'Type dropdown filters learnings by learning_type'
      - 'Agent dropdown filters learnings by target agent'
      - 'Filters compose with status tab (all three params sent to GET endpoint)'
      - 'Clearing a filter removes that param from the query'
  - id: 02-03
    name: 'LearningList, LearningCard, and AgentChips'
    description: >
      Create LearningList to render filtered results as LearningCard components.
      LearningCard shows text, type badge, priority, source, timestamp, and AgentChips.
      Cards are expandable for full text. Empty state shown when no results match.
    files_to_modify:
      - app/src/client/components/learning/LearningList.tsx
      - app/src/client/components/learning/LearningCard.tsx
      - app/src/client/components/learning/AgentChips.tsx
    depends_on:
      - 02-01
      - 02-02
    criteria:
      - 'Each learning renders as a card with text, type, priority, source, and timestamp'
      - 'AgentChips display target agents as colored badges; empty targets show "All agents"'
      - 'Card expands to show full text when truncated'
      - 'Empty state message displayed when filter combination returns no learnings'
      - 'Cards on Pending tab show approve/dismiss action buttons'
- id: '03'
  name: 'Pending Actions and Create Dialog'
  steps:
  - id: 03-01
    name: 'ApproveDialog and DismissDialog for pending learnings'
    description: >
      Create ApproveDialog allowing optional text edit before approval, showing collision warnings.
      Create DismissDialog with required reason field.
      Wire both to use-learning-actions mutations; list refreshes on success.
    files_to_modify:
      - app/src/client/components/learning/ApproveDialog.tsx
      - app/src/client/components/learning/DismissDialog.tsx
    depends_on:
      - 02-03
    criteria:
      - 'Approve dialog opens from pending card action button'
      - 'User can optionally edit text before approving'
      - 'Successful approval transitions learning to active and refreshes list'
      - 'Dismiss dialog requires a reason before submission'
      - 'Successful dismiss transitions learning to dismissed and refreshes list'
  - id: 03-02
    name: 'CreateDialog with type, priority, agent targeting, and collision feedback'
    description: >
      Create CreateDialog form with text, type selector, priority selector, and agent
      multi-select (from KNOWN_LEARNING_TARGET_AGENTS). POST to create endpoint.
      Display collision results returned from server inline in dialog.
    files_to_modify:
      - app/src/client/components/learning/CreateDialog.tsx
    depends_on:
      - 01-01
      - 02-03
    criteria:
      - 'Create dialog accessible from page header button'
      - 'Form validates required fields (text, type) before submission'
      - 'Agent multi-select allows choosing specific agents or leaving empty for all'
      - 'Collision feedback displayed inline when server returns collisions'
      - 'Successful creation refreshes the learning list'
  - id: 03-03
    name: 'Wire create and pending actions into learnings page'
    description: >
      Connect CreateDialog trigger button to page header.
      Connect ApproveDialog and DismissDialog to LearningCard action buttons on Pending tab.
      Ensure all dialog flows update list via use-learnings refetch.
    files_to_modify:
      - app/src/client/routes/learnings-page.tsx
      - app/src/client/components/learning/LearningCard.tsx
    depends_on:
      - 03-01
      - 03-02
    criteria:
      - 'Page header shows "New Learning" button that opens CreateDialog'
      - 'Pending tab cards show Approve and Dismiss buttons that open respective dialogs'
      - 'All dialog completions trigger list refetch without manual page refresh'
      - 'Active tab cards do not show approve/dismiss buttons'
- id: '04'
  name: 'Edit and Deactivate Active Learnings'
  steps:
  - id: 04-01
    name: 'PUT endpoint and updateLearningFields query'
    description: >
      Add handleEdit to learning route handlers for PUT /learnings/:learningId.
      Add updateLearningFields query (text, priority, target_agents) with active-only guard.
      Register PUT route in start-server. Re-embed on text change.
    files_to_modify:
      - app/src/server/learning/learning-route.ts
      - app/src/server/learning/queries.ts
      - app/src/server/runtime/start-server.ts
    depends_on: []
    criteria:
      - 'PUT returns 200 with updated fields on valid edit of active learning'
      - 'PUT returns 409 when learning is not in active status'
      - 'PUT returns 404 for non-existent or wrong-workspace learning'
      - 'PUT returns 400 for empty or whitespace-only text'
      - 'Text edit triggers embedding regeneration'
  - id: 04-02
    name: 'EditDialog and DeactivateDialog for active learnings'
    description: >
      Create EditDialog with editable text, priority, and agent targeting fields.
      Create DeactivateDialog with confirmation prompt.
      Wire both to active-tab card actions; EditDialog calls PUT, DeactivateDialog calls action endpoint.
    files_to_modify:
      - app/src/client/components/learning/EditDialog.tsx
      - app/src/client/components/learning/DeactivateDialog.tsx
      - app/src/client/components/learning/LearningCard.tsx
      - app/src/client/routes/learnings-page.tsx
    depends_on:
      - 04-01
      - 02-03
    criteria:
      - 'Edit dialog opens from active card action button with pre-filled fields'
      - 'Edit dialog submits changed fields via PUT and refreshes list on success'
      - 'Deactivate dialog requires confirmation before submission'
      - 'Successful deactivation moves learning to deactivated status and refreshes list'
      - 'Active tab cards show Edit and Deactivate buttons; other tabs do not'
implementation_scope:
  source_directories:
    - app/src/client/routes/
    - app/src/client/hooks/
    - app/src/client/components/learning/
    - app/src/client/components/layout/
    - app/src/shared/
    - app/src/server/learning/
    - app/src/server/runtime/
  test_directories:
    - tests/acceptance/learning-library/
  excluded_patterns:
    - node_modules/**
    - dist/**
validation:
  status: pending
  reviewer: acceptance-designer
  approved_at: pending

<!-- DES-ENFORCEMENT : exempt -->

Review the roadmap at docs/feature/learning-library/roadmap.yaml for the Learning Library UI feature.

## Review Criteria

1. **Step atomicity**: Each step should be self-contained and independently testable
2. **Dependency correctness**: Dependencies between steps should be accurate and minimal
3. **AC quality**: Acceptance criteria should be behavioral (WHAT not HOW), measurable, and testable
4. **Decomposition**: Steps should modify 1-4 files each (ratio ≤ 2.5)
5. **Completeness**: All files from the architecture design should be covered
6. **Order**: Implementation order should make sense (foundations first, features build on each other)

## Architecture Context

Feature: Learning Library UI — a new page for managing agent behavioral rules.
- 13 new files + 5 modified files = 18 production files
- 4 user stories: Browse & Filter, Inline Pending Actions, Create Learning, Edit/Deactivate
- Backend API is 100% complete except PUT /learnings/:id (new endpoint)
- Frontend: React + TanStack Router + Zustand + vanilla CSS (BEM)
- Paradigm: functional

## Expected File Coverage

**New files that must appear in roadmap:**
- routes/learnings-page.tsx
- hooks/use-learnings.ts, use-learning-actions.ts, use-pending-learning-count.ts
- components/learning/: LearningList, LearningCard, LearningFilters, StatusTabs, AgentChips
- components/learning/: ApproveDialog, DismissDialog, EditDialog, DeactivateDialog, CreateDialog

**Modified files:**
- router.tsx, WorkspaceSidebar.tsx, contracts.ts
- learning-route.ts, queries.ts, start-server.ts

## Verdict

Respond with APPROVED or REJECTED with specific findings. If REJECTED, list specific changes needed.

1→# Learning Library -- Architecture Document 2→ 3→## System Context 4→ 5→The Learning Library is a new UI page within the existing Brain web application. It provides a management interface for agent behavioral rules (learnings). The backend API is 100% complete -- this feature is purely frontend with one backend gap (PUT endpoint for editing active learnings). 6→ 7→### Relationship to Existing System 8→ 9→- **Feed (GovernanceFeed)**: Push notification layer -- surfaces pending learnings as review items. Actions: approve/dismiss only. 10→- **Library (this feature)**: Pull management layer -- full CRUD, filtering, tabbed browsing. Actions: approve/dismiss/edit/deactivate/create. 11→- **Shared**: Both use the same API endpoints and should share dialog components for approve/dismiss flows. 12→ 13→## C4 System Context (L1) 14→ 15→```mermaid 16→graph TB 17→ User["Workspace Owner

type/agent filter"| UseLearnings 83→ UseLearnings -->|"provides data"| List 84→ List -->|"renders"| Card 85→ Card -->|"opens"| ApproveDialog 86→ Card -->|"opens"| DismissDialog 87→ Card -->|"opens"| EditDialog 88→ Card -->|"opens"| DeactivateDialog 89→ ApproveDialog -->|"calls"| UseLearningActions 90→ DismissDialog -->|"calls"| UseLearningActions 91→ EditDialog -->|"calls"| UseLearningActions 92→ DeactivateDialog -->|"calls"| UseLearningActions 93→ CreateDialog -->|"calls"| UseLearningActions 94→ UseLearningActions -->|"refreshes"| UseLearnings 95→``` 96→ 97→## Component Architecture 98→ 99→### New Files 100→ 101→| File | Responsibility | 102→|------|---------------| 103→| `app/src/client/routes/learnings-page.tsx` | Page component, composes hooks + child components | 104→| `app/src/client/hooks/use-learnings.ts` | Data fetching hook: list, filter params, refresh, tab counts | 105→| `app/src/client/hooks/use-learning-actions.ts` | Mutation hook: approve, dismiss, edit, deactivate, create | 106→| `app/src/client/components/learning/LearningList.tsx` | Renders filtered learning cards | 107→| `app/src/client/components/learning/LearningCard.tsx` | Expandable card with status-aware action buttons | 108→| `app/src/client/components/learning/LearningFilters.tsx` | Type + agent filter dropdowns | 109→| `app/src/client/components/learning/StatusTabs.tsx` | Tab bar with counts | 110→| `app/src/client/components/learning/ApproveDialog.tsx` | Approve with optional text edit + collision warning | 111→| `app/src/client/components/learning/DismissDialog.tsx` | Dismiss with required reason | 112→| `app/src/client/components/learning/EditDialog.tsx` | Edit text/type/priority/agents | 113→| `app/src/client/components/learning/DeactivateDialog.tsx` | Deactivation confirmation | 114→| `app/src/client/components/learning/CreateDialog.tsx` | Full creation form with collision feedback | 115→| `app/src/client/components/learning/AgentChips.tsx` | Render target agent badges (shared across cards + dialogs) | 116→ 117→### Modified Files 118→ 119→| File | Change | 120→|------|--------| 121→| `app/src/client/router.tsx` | Add `/learnings` route under `authLayout` | 122→| `app/src/client/components/layout/WorkspaceSidebar.tsx` | Add "Learnings" nav link with pending count badge | 123→| `app/src/shared/contracts.ts` | Add `KNOWN_AGENT_TYPES` constant, `LearningListItem` wire type | 124→| `app/src/server/learning/learning-route.ts` | Add PUT handler for editing active learnings | 125→| `app/src/server/runtime/start-server.ts` | Register PUT route | 126→ 127→### File Count: ~13 new, ~5 modified = ~18 production files 128→ 129→## Data Flow 130→ 131→``` 132→User interaction 133→ --> Component (LearningCard / Dialog) 134→ --> useLearningActions (mutation) 135→ --> fetch() to API endpoint 136→ --> Server validates + persists 137→ <-- Response 138→ --> useLearnings.refresh() (re-fetch list) 139→ <-- UI updates with fresh data 140→``` 141→ 142→### Filter State Flow 143→ 144→``` 145→StatusTabs selection --> useLearnings sets `status` param 146→LearningFilters change --> useLearnings sets `type` / `agent` params 147→URL search params <--> filter state (bidirectional sync via TanStack Router) 148→Combined params --> GET /api/workspaces/:wsId/learnings?status=X&type=Y&agent=Z 149→``` 150→ 151→## Integration Patterns 152→ 153→### Routing Integration 154→ 155→Add route as child of `authLayout` (same pattern as `graphRoute`, `reviewRoute`): 156→ 157→- Path: `/learnings` 158→- Component: `LearningsPage` 159→- No search params validation needed initially (filters can use component state; URL sync is enhancement) 160→ 161→### Sidebar Integration 162→ 163→Add "Learnings" link between "Feed" and "Graph" in `WorkspaceSidebar`: 164→ 165→- Uses `useMatchRoute` for active state (existing pattern) 166→- Pending count badge: derived from a lightweight fetch or piggybacked on the learnings hook when on the page. For other pages, the sidebar does NOT fetch counts independently (avoid extra API calls). Badge shows only when on the learnings page. 167→ 168→**Decision**: Sidebar badge fetches pending count independently via a small dedicated hook (`usePendingLearningCount`) that calls `GET /learnings?status=pending_approval` and reads `learnings.length`. This runs on a 60s poll (half the feed rate). This way the badge is always visible, not just on the learnings page. 169→ 170→### State Management 171→ 172→**No new Zustand store.** The learning library is a self-contained page. State lives in: 173→ 174→1. `useLearnings` hook: `{ learnings, isLoading, error, counts, refresh, filters, setFilters }` 175→2. `useLearningActions` hook: `{ approve, dismiss, edit, deactivate, create, isSubmitting }` 176→3. Component-local state for dialog open/close and form fields 177→ 178→This follows the existing pattern (HomePage uses `useGovernanceFeed`, not a store). 179→ 180→### Dialog Pattern 181→ 182→All dialogs use the native HTML `` element or a simple modal div pattern (whichever the codebase already uses -- inspection shows no existing dialog/modal pattern, so introduce a minimal one). 183→ 184→**Dialog lifecycle**: 185→1. Card action button click sets `openDialog` state: `{ type: "approve" | "dismiss" | "edit" | "deactivate", learning: LearningListItem }` 186→2. Dialog renders conditionally based on state 187→3. Dialog calls `useLearningActions` method on confirm 188→4. On success: close dialog, trigger `refresh()` 189→5. On API failure: dialog stays open, shows inline error message, preserves form state, allows retry 190→6. On cancel: close dialog, no side effects 191→ 192→### Optimistic Updates 193→ 194→**Decision: No optimistic updates.** Rationale: 195→- Solo user, no concurrent writers 196→- API responses are fast (local SurrealDB) 197→- Optimistic updates add complexity (rollback logic, stale state reconciliation) 198→- Simple refetch-on-success is sufficient and matches the feed pattern (`GovernanceFeed` does `onRefresh()` after actions) 199→ 200→### Toast Notifications 201→ 202→No existing toast system found in codebase. Two options: 203→1. Add a minimal toast (CSS animation + setTimeout) 204→2. Use the existing feed pattern of just refreshing the list (visual feedback = item moves/changes) 205→ 206→**Decision**: Defer toast to a future enhancement. Visual feedback from the item changing status/moving tabs is sufficient for MVP. The card will show a brief loading state during mutation. 207→ 208→## Backend Gaps 209→ 210→### 1. PUT /api/workspaces/:workspaceId/learnings/:learningId (NEW) 211→ 212→Edit active learnings: update text, priority, target_agents. The existing `updateLearningText` query function handles text updates. Needs extension for priority and target_agents fields. 213→ 214→**Constraints**: 215→- Only `active` status learnings can be edited 216→- Workspace scope validation required 217→- Re-embed text if changed (collision detection NOT required on edit -- user is intentionally modifying) 218→ 219→### 2. KNOWN_AGENT_TYPES Shared Constant 220→ 221→Currently agent types are scattered: 222→- `AgentType` in `chat/tools/types.ts`: `"code_agent" | "architect" | "management" | "design_partner" | "observer"` (IAM types) 223→- Learnings use different labels: `"chat_agent" | "pm_agent" | "observer_agent" | "mcp"` (target agent types) 224→ 225→These are different concepts. The learning target agents are NOT the same as the IAM agent types. Add to `shared/contracts.ts`: 226→ 227→``` 228→KNOWN_LEARNING_TARGET_AGENTS with display labels 229→``` 230→ 231→### 3. Reactivation Transition (deactivated --> active) 232→ 233→The current `VALID_TRANSITIONS` map does not include a path from `deactivated` back to `active`. The edit/deactivate journey mentions "can be reactivated later" but no reactivation action exists. 234→ 235→**Decision**: Defer reactivation to a follow-up. The UX journeys do not include a reactivation scenario. The deactivated tab is view-only per the shared artifacts registry. When needed, add `reactivate` action to `VALID_TRANSITIONS`. 236→ 237→## Quality Attribute Strategies 238→ 239→| Attribute | Strategy | 240→|-----------|----------| 241→| **Maintainability** | Feature-scoped directory (`components/learning/`), hooks follow existing patterns, no new state management libraries | 242→| **Usability** | Tab-based navigation matches mental model (active/pending/dismissed/deactivated), keyboard accessible, expand-in-place for detail | 243→| **Performance** | Single API call per tab (server-side filtering), no polling on library page (manual refresh only), sidebar badge on 60s poll | 244→| **Testability** | Hooks are pure data-fetching functions, components receive data via props, dialogs are self-contained | 245→| **Consistency** | BEM CSS naming, same fetch pattern as `useGovernanceFeed`, same badge components (`EntityBadge`, `CategoryBadge`) | 246→ 247→## Deployment Architecture 248→ 249→No deployment changes. The learning library is: 250→- A new route in the existing React SPA (served by Bun) 251→- One new server endpoint (PUT) added to the existing route registration 252→- No new infrastructure, no new services, no new databases 253→

    39→## Component Responsibilities
    40→
    41→### Page Layer
    42→
    43→**LearningsPage** (`routes/learnings-page.tsx`)
    44→- Composes `useLearnings` and `useLearningActions` hooks
    45→- Renders page header with "New" button
    46→- Renders `StatusTabs`, `LearningFilters`, `LearningList`
    47→- Manages dialog open/close state: `{ dialog?: { type, learning? } }`
    48→- Renders the active dialog component conditionally
    49→- Passes `refresh` callback from `useLearnings` to action handlers
    50→
    51→### Hook Layer
    52→
    53→**useLearnings** (`hooks/use-learnings.ts`)
    54→- Pattern: `useGovernanceFeed` (useState + useEffect + fetch)
    55→- State: `learnings`, `isLoading`, `error`, `filters` (status, type, agent), `counts`
    56→- On mount + filter change: `GET /api/workspaces/:wsId/learnings?status=X&type=Y&agent=Z`
    57→- Counts: fetched once on mount with no status filter, then derived. OR separate fetch per status. Decision: single unfiltered fetch for counts on mount, filtered fetch for display. Counts update on `refresh()`.
    58→- Exposes: `{ learnings, counts, isLoading, error, filters, setFilters, refresh }`
    59→
    60→**useLearningActions** (`hooks/use-learning-actions.ts`)
    61→- Pattern: stateless functions that return promises
    62→- Each function: validate params -> fetch to API -> return result
    63→- Functions: `approve(id, newText?)`, `dismiss(id, reason)`, `edit(id, fields)`, `deactivate(id)`, `create(fields)`
    64→- Tracks: `isSubmitting` state for loading indicators
    65→- Does NOT call refresh -- caller does (page component chains: action -> refresh)
    66→
    67→**usePendingLearningCount** (`hooks/use-pending-learning-count.ts`)
    68→- Pattern: `useGovernanceFeed` polling pattern (60s interval)
    69→- Calls `GET /api/workspaces/:wsId/learnings?status=pending_approval`
    70→- Returns: `{ count: number, isLoading: boolean }`
    71→- Used by `WorkspaceSidebar` for badge display
    72→
    73→### Component Layer
    74→
    75→**StatusTabs** (`components/learning/StatusTabs.tsx`)
    76→- Props: `{ activeStatus, counts: Record<LearningStatus, number>, onStatusChange }`
    77→- Renders: tab buttons for Active / Pending / Dismissed / Deactivated
    78→- Each tab shows count badge
    79→- Pending tab shows count in accent color when > 0
    80→
    81→**LearningFilters** (`components/learning/LearningFilters.tsx`)
    82→- Props: `{ type, agent, onTypeChange, onAgentChange }`
    83→- Renders: two `<select>` dropdowns
    84→- Type options: "All types" + LEARNING_TYPES from contracts
    85→- Agent options: "All agents" + KNOWN_LEARNING_TARGET_AGENTS from contracts
    86→- Pattern: follows `SearchFilters` component (chip/button style or dropdown)
    87→
    88→**LearningList** (`components/learning/LearningList.tsx`)
    89→- Props: `{ learnings, isLoading, onAction }`
    90→- Renders: list of `LearningCard` components
    91→- Empty state: different messages per tab (empty active vs empty pending vs zero learnings)
    92→- Loading state: skeleton or loading text (follow feed pattern)
    93→
    94→**LearningCard** (`components/learning/LearningCard.tsx`)
    95→- Props: `{ learning: LearningListItem, isExpanded, onToggle, onAction }`
    96→- Collapsed: text preview, type badge (`EntityBadge` reuse), priority badge, agent chips, source
    97→- Expanded: full text, created date, metadata, action buttons
    98→- Action buttons vary by status:
    99→  - `active`: Edit, Deactivate
   100→  - `pending_approval`: Approve, Dismiss (+ suggested_by + confidence display)
   101→  - `dismissed`: view-only (shows dismiss reason + date)
   102→  - `deactivated`: view-only (shows deactivation date)
   103→
   104→**AgentChips** (`components/learning/AgentChips.tsx`)
   105→- Props: `{ agents: string[] }`
   106→- Empty array: renders "All agents" chip
   107→- Non-empty: renders individual agent name chips with display labels from `KNOWN_LEARNING_TARGET_AGENTS`
   108→
   109→### Dialog Layer
   110→
   111→**ApproveDialog** (`components/learning/ApproveDialog.tsx`)
   112→- Props: `{ learning, onConfirm, onCancel, isSubmitting }`
   113→- Form: editable text field (pre-populated), collision warnings (fetched inline or displayed from previous check)
   114→- Confirm button: "Approve as Active"
   115→- Calls: `onConfirm(learningId, editedText?)`
   116→
   117→**DismissDialog** (`components/learning/DismissDialog.tsx`)
   118→- Props: `{ learning, onConfirm, onCancel, isSubmitting }`
   119→- Form: required reason textarea
   120→- Confirm button: disabled until reason non-empty
   121→- Calls: `onConfirm(learningId, reason)`
   122→
   123→**EditDialog** (`components/learning/EditDialog.tsx`)
   124→- Props: `{ learning, onConfirm, onCancel, isSubmitting }`
   125→- Form: text, type selector, priority selector, target agents checkboxes
   126→- All fields pre-populated from learning
   127→- Confirm button: "Save Changes"
   128→- Calls: `onConfirm(learningId, { text?, priority?, target_agents? })`
   129→
   130→**DeactivateDialog** (`components/learning/DeactivateDialog.tsx`)
   131→- Props: `{ learning, onConfirm, onCancel, isSubmitting }`
   132→- Confirmation text showing which agents are affected
   133→- Confirm button: "Deactivate"
   134→- Calls: `onConfirm(learningId)`
   135→
   136→**CreateDialog** (`components/learning/CreateDialog.tsx`)
   137→- Props: `{ onConfirm, onCancel, isSubmitting }`
   138→- Form: text, type selector (required), priority (default medium), agent targeting (all/specific)
   139→- Two-phase: submit -> collision check -> show warnings -> confirm or go back
   140→- Calls: `onConfirm({ text, learning_type, priority, target_agents })`

# DES_METADATA
Step: 01-01
Project: learning-library
Command: /nw:execute

# AGENT_IDENTITY
Agent: nw-functional-software-crafter

# SKILL_LOADING
Before starting TDD phases, read your skill files for methodology guidance.
Skills path: ~/.claude/skills/nw/nw-functional-software-crafter/
Always load at PREPARE: tdd-methodology.md, quality-framework.md
Load on-demand per phase as specified in your Skill Loading Strategy table.

# TASK_CONTEXT
Name: Shared contracts, learnings route, and data hooks
Description: Add KNOWN_LEARNING_TARGET_AGENTS constant to shared contracts. Register /learnings route in TanStack Router under authLayout. Create use-learnings hook (fetch + filter state) and use-learning-actions hook (mutations).

Files to modify:
- app/src/shared/contracts.ts
- app/src/client/router.tsx
- app/src/client/hooks/use-learnings.ts
- app/src/client/hooks/use-learning-actions.ts

Acceptance Criteria:
- KNOWN_LEARNING_TARGET_AGENTS exported from contracts with all agent types
- Navigating to /learnings renders a page component without errors
- use-learnings hook fetches learnings from GET endpoint with status/type/agent params
- use-learning-actions hook exposes approve, dismiss, deactivate, create, and edit mutations
- Edit mutation calls PUT endpoint; other mutations call existing POST endpoints

Dependencies: none

Existing acceptance tests: tests/acceptance/learning-library/walking-skeleton.test.ts, tests/acceptance/learning-library/milestone-1-browse-filter.test.ts

## Key Patterns to Follow

**Router pattern** (from existing router.tsx):
- Use `createRoute({ getParentRoute: () => authLayout, path: '/learnings', component: LearningsPage })`
- Add to routeTree in router

**Hook pattern** (from existing useGovernanceFeed):
- useState + useEffect + fetch (NO React Query)
- Return `{ data, isLoading, error, refresh }`
- Use `useWorkspaceState(s => s.workspaceId)` for workspace context

**Contracts** (from existing shared/contracts.ts):
- Export typed constant array: `KNOWN_LEARNING_TARGET_AGENTS`
- Each entry: `{ value: string, label: string }`
- Values: "chat_agent", "pm_agent", "observer_agent", "mcp"

**API endpoints:**
- GET /api/workspaces/:wsId/learnings?status=X&type=Y&agent=Z (list)
- POST /api/workspaces/:wsId/learnings (create with collision detection)
- POST /api/workspaces/:wsId/learnings/:id/actions (approve/dismiss/deactivate)
- PUT /api/workspaces/:wsId/learnings/:id (edit — to be built in step 04-01)

**Existing LearningSummary type** (in contracts.ts):
Check if LearningSummary already exists in contracts.ts. If so, reuse it. If not, add it.

**Data value contract**: Never use `null`. Use `undefined` via optional properties.
**Paradigm**: Functional — pure functions, composition, types as documentation.

# TDD_PHASES
Execute in order:
0. PREPARE - Load context, verify prerequisites, read existing files
1. RED_ACCEPTANCE - Write failing acceptance test (or verify existing tests fail as expected)
2. RED_UNIT - Write failing unit test for the hooks/contracts
3. GREEN - Minimal code to pass tests
   After GREEN: run FULL test suite. If all pass, proceed to COMMIT immediately.
   Never move to new task or stop without committing green code.
4. COMMIT - Stage and commit with conventional message
   Include git trailer: `Step-ID: 01-01` (required for DES verification)

# QUALITY_GATES
- All tests pass before COMMIT
- No skipped phases without blocked_by reason
- Coverage maintained or improved

# OUTCOME_RECORDING
After ACTUALLY EXECUTING each phase, record via DES CLI:

    PYTHONPATH=$HOME/.claude/lib/python python -m des.cli.log_phase \
      --project-dir docs/feature/learning-library \
      --step-id 01-01 \
      --phase {PHASE_NAME} \
      --status EXECUTED \
      --data PASS

For SKIPPED phases:

    PYTHONPATH=$HOME/.claude/lib/python python -m des.cli.log_phase \
      --project-dir docs/feature/learning-library \
      --step-id 01-01 \
      --phase {PHASE_NAME} \
      --status SKIPPED \
      --data "NOT_APPLICABLE: reason"

# RECORDING_INTEGRITY
- Only record phases you actually executed
- Use DES CLI exclusively — never manually edit execution-log.yaml
- Each phase must be recorded immediately after execution
- Orchestrator never writes phase entries — only the executing agent

# BOUNDARY_RULES
- Only modify files listed in files_to_modify (plus test files)
- Do not load roadmap.yaml
- Do not modify execution-log.yaml structure (append only via CLI)
- NEVER write execution-log entries for phases you did not execute
- Use `--no-verify` and `-s` flags when committing (per AGENTS.md)

# TIMEOUT_INSTRUCTION
Target: 30 turns max. If approaching limit, COMMIT current progress.
If GREEN complete (all tests pass), MUST commit before returning.

1→# FP Principles
     2→
     3→Core functional programming thinking patterns. Language-agnostic.
     4→
     5→Cross-references: [fp-domain-modeling](./fp-domain-modeling.md) | [fp-hexagonal-architecture](./fp-hexagonal-architecture.md) | [fp-algebra-driven-design](./fp-algebra-driven-design.md)
     6→
     7→---
     8→
     9→## 1. Higher-Order Functions as Problem Decomposition
    10→
    11→[STARTER]
    12→
    13→Three operations replace most loops:
    14→
    15→| Operation | Purpose | Replaces |
    16→|-----------|---------|----------|
    17→| **Map** | Transform each element, preserve structure | Loop building new collection |
    18→| **Filter** | Keep elements matching condition | Loop with conditional |
    19→| **Fold** | Accumulate elements into single result | Loop with running total |
    20→
    21→**When to use Map**: Transform every element without changing collection shape. Nested maps handle nested structures.
    22→**When to use Filter**: Select elements without changing their values.
    23→**When to use Fold**: Reduce collection to single value. Accumulator IS your state. Combining function IS your state transition. Folds make state machines explicit.
    24→
    25→**Decision**: "Am I transforming, selecting, or accumulating?" Pick matching operation. If none fit, compose two.
    26→
    27→**Why**: These operations communicate intent. Map says "same shape, different values." Fold says "many inputs, one output." Loops say nothing about intent until you read every line.
    28→
    29→---
    30→
    31→## 2. Type-Driven Design
    32→
    33→[STARTER]
    34→
    35→Write the type signature before implementation. The type tells you what the function can and cannot do.
    36→
    37→**Process**:
    38→1. Declare what the function consumes and produces
    39→2. Ask: "which type-specific operations do I actually use?"
    40→3. Replace concrete types with type variables for everything you don't inspect
    41→4. Add constraints only for capabilities you use (equality, ordering, display)
    42→
    43→**Design progression**: Concrete types -> type variables -> constrained type variables. Each step increases reuse while documenting minimal assumptions.
    44→
    45→**Why**: Function's type signature is a contract. Narrower types mean fewer possible implementations, fewer bugs.
    46→
    47→---
    48→
    49→## 3. Pattern Matching as Decision Decomposition
    50→
    51→[STARTER]
    52→
    53→Decompose decisions by data shape, not boolean conditions. Each clause handles one concrete case. Compiler verifies exhaustiveness.
    54→
    55→**When to use pattern matching**: "What shape is this data?"
    56→**When to use guards/conditions**: "What property does this value have?"
    57→**When to use named bindings**: Intermediate results need a name to avoid repetition.
    58→
    59→**Heuristic**: Prefer small extracted functions over giant match expressions. Pattern match on top-level shape, delegate to named functions for sub-decisions.
    60→
    61→**Exhaustiveness as safety net**: When you add a new variant to a choice type, compiler flags every match that doesn't handle it.
    62→
    63→---
    64→
    65→## 4. Composition Patterns
    66→
    67→[INTERMEDIATE]
    68→
    69→### Partial Application
    70→
    71→Fix some arguments of a general function to create specialized version. Eliminates throwaway helper functions.
    72→
    73→**When**: General function exists and you need specialized version for specific context.
    74→
    75→### Function Composition (Pipelines)
    76→
    77→Chain functions where output of one feeds into next. Each function has single responsibility.
    78→
    79→**Why**: Composition reveals architecture of computation. Pipelines read as sequence of steps, making business process visible.
    80→
    81→### Point-Free Style
    82→
    83→Omit explicit argument when function is just a composition. Use when it reveals intent. Avoid when it obscures meaning.
    84→
    85→---
    86→
    87→## 5. Container Abstractions
    88→
    89→[INTERMEDIATE] -> [ADVANCED]
    90→
    91→Progressive hierarchy for working with values inside containers (nullables, lists, futures, results).
    92→
    93→### [INTERMEDIATE] Transformable Container (Functor)
    94→
    95→**What**: Apply function to values inside container without changing structure.
    96→**Plain English**: "I have a value in a box. Transform the value without opening the box."
    97→**When**: You have nullable/optional/list/future and want to transform contents without inspecting the container.
    98→**Guarantees**: Transforming with identity does nothing. Can fuse or split transformations freely.
    99→
   100→### [INTERMEDIATE] Combinable Containers (Applicative)
   101→
   102→**What**: Apply a function inside a container to values inside other containers.
   103→**Plain English**: "I have a function in a box AND values in boxes. Combine them."
   104→**When**: Validation -- check multiple fields independently, combine results only if all succeed. Doesn't short-circuit; collects all errors.
   105→
   106→### [INTERMEDIATE] Combinable Values (Monoid)
   107→
   108→**What**: Combine two values of same type into one, with default element that changes nothing.
   109→**Plain English**: "I have many values. Smash them together into one."
   110→**When**: Folding/reducing collections. Combining operation must be associative, enabling parallelism.
   111→**Examples**: String concatenation with empty string | addition with zero | list append with empty list.
   112→
   113→### [ADVANCED] Chainable Operations (Monad)
   114→
   115→**What**: Chain operations where each step produces wrapped value, next step depends on previous result.
   116→**Plain English**: "Step 1's output determines what step 2 does. Each step might fail/branch/have effects."
   117→**When**: Sequential dependent operations where each step can fail, branch, or produce effects.
   118→
   119→### Decision Tree: Which Abstraction Do I Need?
   120→
   121→```
   122→Do I need to transform values inside a container?
   123→  YES, one function, one container --> Transformable (Functor)
   124→  YES, combine multiple independent containers --> Combinable Containers (Applicative)
   125→  YES, chain dependent operations sequentially --> Chainable Operations (Monad)
   126→Do I need to combine values of the same type?
   127→  YES --> Combinable Values (Monoid)
   128→```
   129→
   130→### Progression Summary
   131→
   132→Each level adds a new kind of combination:
   133→- **Transformable**: one function, one container
   134→- **Combinable Containers**: one function, multiple containers (independent)
   135→- **Chainable**: sequential dependent operations, each producing container
   136→- **Combinable Values**: same-type values collapsed into one
   137→
   138→### Runnable Example: Map, Filter, Fold on Domain Objects
   139→
   140→```
   141→orders = [Order(100, "pending"), Order(250, "shipped"), Order(50, "pending")]
   142→
   143→pendingTotals = orders
   144→  |> filter (o -> o.status == "pending")    -- [Order(100, "pending"), Order(50, "pending")]
   145→  |> map (o -> o.amount)                    -- [100, 50]
   146→  |> fold 0 (acc, x -> acc + x)            -- 150
   147→```
   148→
   149→---
   150→
   151→## 6. Specialized Chainable Patterns
   152→
   153→[ADVANCED]
   154→
   155→| Pattern | What It Manages | When to Use |
   156→|---------|----------------|-------------|
   157→| **Optional** (Maybe/Option) | Possible absence | Operations that can fail without explanation |
   158→| **Result** (Either) | Failure with context | Operations that fail with error details |
   159→| **Environment** (Reader) | Shared read-only config | Dependency injection, configuration threading |
   160→| **Accumulator** (Writer) | Side-channel output | Logging, auditing, collecting metadata |
   161→| **Stateful** (State) | Sequential state changes | Counters, parsers, accumulators |
   162→
   163→These compose: real applications stack multiple patterns. See [fp-hexagonal-architecture](./fp-hexagonal-architecture.md) for DI patterns.
   164→
   165→---
   166→
   167→## 7. Lazy Evaluation as Design Pattern
   168→
   169→[INTERMEDIATE]
   170→
   171→Separate WHAT to compute from WHEN it gets computed. Define potentially infinite sequences and let consumer determine how much to evaluate.
   172→
   173→**When**: Generating candidates then selecting results | pagination and streaming | decoupling producers from consumers | build systems that only rebuild what changed.
   174→
   175→**Separation principle**: Generate all possibilities, then filter. Declarative style says WHAT you want, not HOW to search.
   176→
   177→---
   178→
   179→## 8. The FP Problem-Solving Method
   180→
   181→[STARTER]
   182→
   183→1. **Start with type signature**: What does this function consume and produce?
   184→2. **Identify traversal pattern**: Map, filter, fold, or search?
   185→3. **Recognize accumulator**: If folding, what is the state and how does each element change it?
   186→4. **Decompose by data shape**: Pattern match on constructors, handle each case independently
   187→5. **Compose small functions**: Build complex behavior from simple, tested pieces
   188→
   189→**Mindset shift**: Describe WHAT to compute (transformations, compositions, constraints) rather than HOW (loops, mutations, control flow).
   190→
   191→| Imperative Thinking | Functional Thinking |
   192→|---------------------|---------------------|
   193→| Loop through items | Map/filter/fold over collections |
   194→| Mutate variables | Transform immutable values |
   195→| Check conditions with if/else | Pattern match on data shapes |
   196→| Inherit from base class | Satisfy capability constraints |
   197→| Call methods on objects | Compose functions into pipelines |
   198→| Handle errors with try/catch | Use Optional/Result for explicit failure in types |
   199→| Pass dependencies explicitly | Use Environment pattern for implicit config |
   200→

<!-- DES-VALIDATION : required -->
<!-- DES-PROJECT-ID : learning-library -->
<!-- DES-STEP-ID : 01-02 -->

# DES_METADATA
Step: 01-02
Project: learning-library
Command: /nw:execute

# AGENT_IDENTITY
Agent: nw-functional-software-crafter

# SKILL_LOADING
Before starting TDD phases, read your skill files for methodology guidance.
Skills path: ~/.claude/skills/nw/nw-functional-software-crafter/
Always load at PREPARE: tdd-methodology.md, quality-framework.md

# TASK_CONTEXT
Name: Sidebar navigation link with pending count badge
Description: Add Learnings nav link to WorkspaceSidebar. Create use-pending-learning-count hook that polls pending_approval count on 60s interval. Display count badge on sidebar link when pending > 0.

Files to modify:
- app/src/client/components/layout/WorkspaceSidebar.tsx
- app/src/client/hooks/use-pending-learning-count.ts

Acceptance Criteria:
- Sidebar shows Learnings link that navigates to /learnings
- Active route highlights the Learnings link
- Hook polls GET /learnings?status=pending_approval on 60s interval
- Badge displays pending count when workspace has pending learnings
- Badge hidden when pending count is zero

Dependencies: 01-01 (completed)

## Key Patterns
- Read WorkspaceSidebar.tsx to understand existing nav link patterns
- Use useMatchRoute for active state (existing sidebar pattern)
- Hook: useState + useEffect + fetch with setInterval for polling
- Use useWorkspaceState(s => s.workspaceId) for workspace context
- Never use null — use undefined via optional properties
- Paradigm: functional

# TDD_PHASES
Execute in order:
0. PREPARE - Load context, read existing sidebar component
1. RED_ACCEPTANCE - Write or verify failing acceptance test
2. RED_UNIT - Write failing unit test for the polling hook
3. GREEN - Minimal code to pass tests. After GREEN: run FULL test suite.
4. COMMIT - Stage and commit with `Step-ID: 01-02` trailer. Use `--no-verify -s` flags.

# QUALITY_GATES
- All tests pass before COMMIT

# OUTCOME_RECORDING
After ACTUALLY EXECUTING each phase, record via DES CLI:
    PYTHONPATH=$HOME/.claude/lib/python python -m des.cli.log_phase \
      --project-dir docs/feature/learning-library \
      --step-id 01-02 \
      --phase {PHASE_NAME} \
      --status EXECUTED \
      --data PASS

For SKIPPED phases:
    PYTHONPATH=$HOME/.claude/lib/python python -m des.cli.log_phase \
      --project-dir docs/feature/learning-library \
      --step-id 01-02 \
      --phase {PHASE_NAME} \
      --status SKIPPED \
      --data "NOT_APPLICABLE: reason"

# RECORDING_INTEGRITY
- Only record phases you actually executed
- Use DES CLI exclusively — never manually edit execution-log.yaml
- Each phase must be recorded immediately after execution
- Orchestrator never writes phase entries — only the executing agent

# BOUNDARY_RULES
- Only modify files listed in files_to_modify (plus test files)
- Do not load roadmap.yaml
- Use `--no-verify` and `-s` flags when committing (per AGENTS.md)

# TIMEOUT_INSTRUCTION
Target: 30 turns max. If approaching limit, COMMIT current progress.

- `DismissDialog.onConfirm(learningId, reason)` -- reason is always present (validation enforces non-empty)
- The server already accepts `new_text` on approve and `reason` on dismiss in the action request body; the `use-learning-actions` hook's `buildActionRequest` will need updating in a subsequent step to pass these fields through

Command: /nw:execute

# AGENT_IDENTITY
Agent: nw-functional-software-crafter

# SKILL_LOADING
Before starting TDD phases, read your skill files for methodology guidance.
Skills path: ~/.claude/skills/nw/nw-functional-software-crafter/
Always load at PREPARE: tdd-methodology.md, quality-framework.md

# TASK_CONTEXT
Name: EditDialog and DeactivateDialog for active learnings
Description: Create EditDialog with editable text, priority, and agent targeting fields. Create DeactivateDialog with confirmation prompt. Wire both to active-tab card actions; EditDialog calls PUT, DeactivateDialog calls action endpoint.

Files to modify:
- app/src/client/components/learning/EditDialog.tsx
- app/src/client/components/learning/DeactivateDialog.tsx
- app/src/client/components/learning/LearningCard.tsx
- app/src/client/routes/learnings-page.tsx

Acceptance Criteria:
- Edit dialog opens from active card action button with pre-filled fields
- Edit dialog submits changed fields via PUT and refreshes list on success
- Deactivate dialog requires confirmation before submission
- Successful deactivation moves learning to deactivated status and refreshes list
- Active tab cards show Edit and Deactivate buttons; other tabs do not

Dependencies: 04-01 (completed — PUT endpoint), 02-03 (completed — cards exist)

## Key Patterns
- Read current learnings-page.tsx to see how dialog state management works (from 03-03)
- Read page-dialog-logic.ts to see dialog state types — extend to support "edit" and "deactivate" actions
- Read use-learning-actions.ts for edit(id, fields) and deactivate(id) mutation signatures
- EditDialog props: { learning, onConfirm, onCancel, isSubmitting }
  - Form: text textarea, priority select, agent targeting checkboxes (all pre-populated)
  - Calls: onConfirm(learningId, { text?, priority?, target_agents? })
  - Uses PUT endpoint via useLearningActions.edit()
- DeactivateDialog props: { learning, onConfirm, onCancel, isSubmitting }
  - Shows confirmation text + which agents affected
  - Calls: onConfirm(learningId)
  - Uses POST action endpoint via useLearningActions.deactivate()
- Extract validation logic to pure functions (edit-dialog-logic.ts or extend dialog-logic.ts)
- Wire into page: edit/deactivate card actions open respective dialogs
- CSS: vanilla CSS with BEM naming
- Paradigm: functional

# TDD_PHASES
Execute in order:
0. PREPARE - Load context, read existing files
1. RED_ACCEPTANCE - Write or verify failing test
2. RED_UNIT - Write failing unit test for edit dialog validation logic
3. GREEN - Minimal code to pass tests. After GREEN: run FULL test suite.
4. COMMIT - Stage and commit with `Step-ID: 04-02` trailer. Use `--no-verify -s` flags.

# QUALITY_GATES
- All tests pass before COMMIT

# OUTCOME_RECORDING
After ACTUALLY EXECUTING each phase, record via DES CLI:
    PYTHONPATH=$HOME/.claude/lib/python python -m des.cli.log_phase \
      --project-dir docs/feature/learning-library \
      --step-id 04-02 \
      --phase {PHASE_NAME} \
      --status EXECUTED \
      --data PASS
For SKIPPED phases:
    PYTHONPATH=$HOME/.claude/lib/python python -m des.cli.log_phase \
      --project-dir docs/feature/learning-library \
      --step-id 04-02 \
      --phase {PHASE_NAME} \
      --status SKIPPED \
      --data "NOT_APPLICABLE: reason"

# RECORDING_INTEGRITY
- Only record phases you actually executed
- Use DES CLI exclusively — never manually edit execution-log.yaml

# BOUNDARY_RULES
- Only modify files listed in files_to_modify (plus test files)
- Do not load roadmap.yaml
- Use `--no-verify` and `-s` flags when committing

# TIMEOUT_INSTRUCTION
Target: 30 turns max. If approaching limit, COMMIT current progress.

Only changed fields are included in the payload.
- `/Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain-v1/karachi/app/src/client/components/learning/EditDialog.tsx` -- Edit dialog component with pre-populated text textarea, priority select, and agent targeting checkboxes. Submit button disabled when no changes detected or text is empty. Calls `useLearningActions.edit()` via PUT endpoint.
- `/Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain-v1/karachi/app/src/client/components/learning/DeactivateDialog.tsx` -- Deactivate confirmation dialog showing the learning text and affected agents. Calls `useLearningActions.deactivate()` via POST action endpoint.
- `/Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain-v1/karachi/tests/unit/learning-library/edit-dialog-logic.test.ts` -- 13 unit tests covering form state construction, validation, dirty-checking, and payload building.

**Modified files:**

- `/Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain-v1/karachi/app/src/client/routes/learnings-page.tsx` -- Wired `EditDialog` and `DeactivateDialog` with handlers that call `edit()`/`deactivate()`, close dialog, and refresh list on success.
- `/Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain-v1/karachi/tests/unit/learning-library/page-dialog-logic.test.ts` -- Added tests for edit and deactivate card actions resolving to correct dialog states.
